spec_match.rationale: Walks the click → permission prompt → `activate` (or `error`) lifecycle the spec defines, using a real `<usermedia>` element under the hood.

interactive.rationale: Step buttons, animated timeline, live event log, and a granted/denied path selector — the visitor drives the demo through both branches without a real device.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Promises animated timeline + event log for both granted and denied paths; both are delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: The `error` path is exercised, but only one error variant. The element can surface `NotAllowedError`, `NotFoundError`, `OverconstrainedError`, `SecurityError` — only the first is shown. Also no demo of repeated activation after a previous error (the recovery story).

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, no glyph icons, button colour overrides paired with background.

openQuestions.title: Cover the four DOMException flavours on error
openQuestions.detail: Spec lists NotAllowedError, NotFoundError, OverconstrainedError, SecurityError. Add a dropdown / tab to walk through each with the matching timeline, since they imply different recovery UX.

openQuestions.title: Demo recovery on second click
openQuestions.detail: Following a denied path, click the element again. Either the browser surfaces a `try again` UI or the click is ignored. Show which actually happens — this is the explainer's headline benefit over the JS API.

openQuestions.title: Surface `event.target.stream` directly in the log
openQuestions.detail: The activate event carries the stream via `event.target.stream`. The log currently abstracts the value behind a step description. Show the live stringified MediaStream so a developer sees what they get.

summary: Clear lifecycle visualisation — exactly what beginners need. Add the chromestatus link and stretch the error path to cover the four DOMException variants and the demo becomes the canonical reference.
